Name: Noxel360 Core
Subtitle: The unified dashboard that hosts every NOXEL module.
Description: Noxel360 Core is the foundation layer: the app shell, navigation, permissions-ready structure, audit history, and export systems that keep every module consistent and upgradeable.
Highlights: Exports, history, and shared UI tokens

Features.Title: App shell & routing
Features.Text: A single, cohesive framework that standardizes navigation, layouts, and module entry points.

Features.Title: Permissions-ready structure
Features.Text: Designed for roles and workspace separation, with auditability in mind.

Features.Title: Export foundation
Features.Text: Shared export utilities and report templates used by all modules.

UseCases.Title: Agency multi-client ops
UseCases.Text: Keep every client's workflow consistent with predictable dashboards and exports.

UseCases.Title: In-house standardization
UseCases.Text: Centralize tools, history, and reporting into one operational hub.

Faq.Q: Do I need Core to use modules?
Faq.A: Yes. Core hosts the dashboard, navigation, and shared services.
Yes. Noxel360 is modular by design. Start small and scale up.

Name: NOXEL MAESTRO
Subtitle: Login + unified preferences that power future Morph personalization.
Description: NOXEL MAESTRO is the backbone for identity, consent-first preferences, and cross-site continuity. It enables a user to carry visual and language preferences across all Noxel-powered experiences, only when explicitly provided.

Features.Title: Unified identity layer
Features.Text: Central account plus future SSO across NOXEL modules.

Features.Title: Preference store
Features.Text: Language, UI choices, and settings stored for continuity across experiences.

Features.Title: Consent by design
Features.Text: Preferences are user-provided. Easy reset and transparency built in.

UseCases.Title: Multi-site clients
UseCases.Text: Offer a consistent, personalized UX across NOXEL-powered properties.

UseCases.Title: Bilingual-first businesses
UseCases.Text: Store language preference and reduce friction for returning users.

Faq.A: No. Preferences are voluntary and consent-first by design.
Yes. A simple reset is part of the Maestro/Morph design.

Name: NOXEL ATLAS
Subtitle: Multi-level world map with overlays, segmentation, and insight layers.
Description: NOXEL ATLAS is your geographic intelligence engine: hierarchical map navigation (continent -> country -> region -> city) with overlays for audience reach, language, and future behavior signals, built for analysis and storytelling.
Highlights: Built to integrate with SEO and Audience Intelligence

Features.Title: Progressive zoom
Features.Text: Continent -> country -> province/region -> city navigation.

Features.Title: Overlay engine
Features.Text: Stackable overlays for audience, language, and future intelligence signals.

Features.Title: Analysis mode
Features.Text: Compare, filter, and export insights for reporting and planning.

UseCases.Title: Market selection
UseCases.Text: Choose where to expand based on data layers and reachable audience.

UseCases.Title: Local SEO planning
UseCases.Text: Connect geography to performance and opportunities.

Faq.A: No. It's broader: audience mapping, segmentation, and insight overlays.

Faq.Q: Will Atlas integrate with other modules?
Faq.A: Yes. It's designed to plug into SEO and future Audience Intelligence.

Name: NOXEL LINKS
Subtitle: Backlinks, internal links, broken links, and opportunity signals.
Description: NOXEL LINKS analyzes link structure and quality signals across your site and beyond. It helps you find broken links, weak internal pathways, and backlink opportunities, with outputs designed for action and reporting.
Highlights: Internal link mapping and anchor analysis

Features.Title: Broken link auditing
Features.Text: Detect broken internal/external links and prioritize fixes.

Features.Title: Internal linking map
Features.Text: Identify orphan pages and strengthen topical clusters.

Features.Title: Anchor analysis
Features.Text: Understand anchor patterns and improve relevance and clarity.

UseCases.Title: Technical cleanup
UseCases.Text: Fix 404s, reduce crawl waste, and improve user navigation.

UseCases.Title: SEO architecture
UseCases.Text: Strengthen topical clusters with internal link strategy.

Faq.A: Not necessarily. It's built to be actionable and integrated with NOXEL workflows.
That's planned as an advanced layer once the base workflow is stable.

Name: NOXEL SEO
Subtitle: Audits, issues, scoring, and fix workflows built for action.
Description: NOXEL SEO scans pages, produces structured issues, explains impact, and recommends fixes. It's designed to pair naturally with SERP and Vitals add-ons without changing your workflow.

Features.Title: Audit engine
Features.Text: Crawl + checks produce consistent issues and scoring.

Features.Title: Fix guidance
Features.Text: Impact-aware fixes that focus on outcomes, not noise.

Features.Title: History & comparisons
Features.Text: Track progress over time and compare scans.

UseCases.Title: Client reporting
UseCases.Text: Generate clear, client-ready reports and next actions.

UseCases.Title: Continuous improvement
UseCases.Text: Keep SEO work steady with scanning + history.

Faq.A: SERP is a separate add-on module. SEO is the base layer.

Faq.Q: Does it work for any site?
Faq.A: Yes. You can scan public URLs and generate issues.

Name: NOXEL SERP
Subtitle: Track rankings, queries, and search visibility over time.
Description: NOXEL SERP focuses on visibility: keywords, ranking movement, and performance signals. Designed as a companion to NOXEL SEO so you can connect issues to results.

Features.Title: Ranking movement
Features.Text: Track progress and detect drops early.

Features.Title: Query grouping
Features.Text: Organize keywords by intent and topic clusters.

Features.Title: Reporting views
Features.Text: Create snapshots and exports for sharing.

UseCases.Title: Prove impact
UseCases.Text: Show how fixes correlate with ranking movement.

UseCases.Title: Early warning system
UseCases.Text: Spot visibility drops before revenue is affected.

Faq.Q: Do I need NOXEL SEO first?
Faq.A: Recommended. SEO provides the fix workflow, SERP validates the outcome.

Faq.Q: Is this a replacement for paid rank tools?
Faq.A: It can be complementary. Noxel's value is workflow integration.

Name: NOXEL VITALS
Subtitle: Vitals-style insights: performance, UX, and reliability signals.
Description: NOXEL VITALS focuses on performance and user experience signals. It complements NOXEL SEO by bringing speed, UX, and reliability into a single fixable workflow.

Features.Title: Vitals-style scoring
Features.Text: Track key performance signals over time.

Features.Title: Actionable fixes
Features.Text: Translate metrics into clear next steps.

Features.Title: History & monitoring
Features.Text: Spot regressions early and keep performance stable.

UseCases.Title: E-commerce speed wins
UseCases.Text: Improve conversion by reducing friction and delays.

UseCases.Title: Audit + fix cadence
UseCases.Text: Keep performance from degrading after releases.

Faq.A: It complements them. Noxel's goal is actionable workflow inside your platform.
Yes, but best results come from pairing performance + SEO together.

Name: NOXEL OPTIMA
Subtitle: WebP conversion, compression, and cleanup workflows (TinyPNG-style direction).
Description: NOXEL OPTIMA focuses on image optimization: convert, compress, and clean assets for performance and quality. Designed to support future SVG tooling and bulk workflows.
Highlights: Built for bulk operations and exports

Features.Title: WebP conversion
Features.Text: Convert PNG/JPG to WebP for performance gains.

Features.Title: Compression workflow
Features.Text: Reduce file size while preserving visual quality.

Features.Title: Bulk processing
Features.Text: Handle multiple files and export results cleanly.

UseCases.Title: Performance improvements
UseCases.Text: Shrink images to improve speed and UX.

UseCases.Title: Asset pipeline
UseCases.Text: Standardize image output across teams and clients.

Faq.A: Not yet. Planned as an advanced tool once workflows are stable.
Yes. Bulk processing is a core direction for this module.

Name: NOXEL FLOW
Subtitle: Theme distribution and modular storefront experiences (Simplexity by NOXEL FLOW).
Description: NOXEL FLOW is the distribution layer for themes and layouts. It standardizes how design systems, templates, and future Morph adaptation ship across projects.
Highlights: Designed to integrate with Morph adaptation

Features.Title: Theme packaging
Features.Text: Ship consistent templates and UI building blocks.

Features.Title: Tokenized UI system
Features.Text: Colors, typography, spacing, standardized and portable.

Features.Title: Module-ready layout zones
Features.Text: Layouts built to host modules cleanly as they arrive.

UseCases.Title: Launch faster
UseCases.Text: Deploy consistent experiences across clients without rebuilding.

UseCases.Title: Boutique styling
UseCases.Text: Deliver premium visual identity with reliable structure.

Faq.A: Simplexity is a flagship theme distributed through NOXEL FLOW.
Flow is designed to work with Maestro/translation layers as you expand.

Name: NOXEL MORPH
Subtitle: Consent-first visual adaptation across NOXEL FLOW themes (future-forward).
Description: NOXEL MORPH applies user-provided preferences (colors, fonts, language, background choices) to NOXEL FLOW themes, without changing site structure or images. Built on consent and transparent preference control.

Features.Title: Preference application engine
Features.Text: Apply color/typography/background tokens across themes safely.

Features.Title: Morph consent controls
Features.Text: User-defined preferences with clear reset and transparency.

Features.Title: Cross-site continuity
Features.Text: Carry preferences across Noxel-powered experiences (opt-in).

UseCases.Title: Personalized boutique UX
UseCases.Text: Let users choose the visual vibe that fits them, consent-first.

UseCases.Title: Accessibility and comfort
UseCases.Text: Enable readable fonts, contrast, and language choices.

Faq.Q: Does Morph change images or layout?
Faq.A: No. It adapts tokens (colors, text, fonts, backgrounds) without structural disruption.
Only if the user explicitly provides preferences (consent-first).
